titles.title: Systems Administrator
titles.description: Maintains and troubleshoots servers, operating systems, user accounts, and core system services to keep IT environments running reliably. Handles routine patching, backups, monitoring, and escalation of system issues.

titles.title: IT Support Specialist
titles.description: Provides day-to-day technical help to end users with hardware, software, account, and connectivity problems. Responds to tickets, resolves common issues, and escalates more complex incidents.

titles.title: Information Security Manager
titles.description: Leads a team or function responsible for protecting systems and data through security controls, monitoring, and incident response. Oversees security policies, risk reduction efforts, and compliance activities.

titles.title: Desktop Support Specialist
titles.description: Supports employee workstations, laptops, printers, and standard business applications. Installs software, configures devices, and resolves user issues at the desktop level.

titles.title: IT Operations Manager
titles.description: Oversees the daily operation of IT services, infrastructure, and support processes. Coordinates monitoring, incident handling, maintenance windows, and service reliability improvements.

titles.title: Network Systems Administrator
titles.description: Administers network-connected systems and supporting infrastructure such as servers, switches, routers, and related services. Monitors performance, applies updates, and resolves connectivity or configuration issues.

titles.title: IT Project Manager
titles.description: Plans and manages IT projects from initiation through delivery, including scope, schedule, budget, and stakeholder communication. Tracks milestones, risks, and dependencies to ensure technology work is completed successfully.

titles.title: Cybersecurity Manager
titles.description: Manages cybersecurity operations, controls, and staff responsible for protecting systems and data. Oversees threat response, security tooling, and implementation of security requirements.

titles.title: IT Infrastructure Manager
titles.description: Leads teams responsible for servers, networks, storage, cloud, and other foundational technology services. Ensures infrastructure is available, secure, and aligned with business needs.

titles.title: Network Administrator
titles.description: Configures and maintains network devices and services such as routers, switches, firewalls, and VPNs. Monitors network health, troubleshoots outages, and applies changes to keep connectivity stable.

titles.title: Cybersecurity Analyst
titles.description: Monitors security alerts, investigates suspicious activity, and helps identify vulnerabilities or threats. Supports incident response, log analysis, and security reporting.

titles.title: Network Operations Manager
titles.description: Oversees the team and processes that monitor and maintain enterprise networks. Manages outages, performance issues, maintenance activities, and operational improvements.

titles.title: Desktop Support Technician
titles.description: Provides hands-on support for user devices, peripherals, and common software problems. Sets up equipment, performs repairs or replacements, and resolves basic technical issues.

titles.title: Chief Information Security Officer
titles.description: Sets the organization’s overall information security strategy and governance direction. Leads security leadership, risk management, policy development, and executive reporting.

titles.title: IT Program Manager
titles.description: Manages a portfolio or program of related IT projects and initiatives. Coordinates multiple teams, budgets, schedules, and dependencies to deliver broader technology outcomes.

titles.title: IT Systems Administrator
titles.description: Maintains servers, operating systems, accounts, and core IT services used across the organization. Performs patching, monitoring, backups, and troubleshooting for system reliability.

titles.title: Windows Systems Administrator
titles.description: Administers Windows servers, Active Directory, Group Policy, and related Microsoft infrastructure. Handles patching, account management, server maintenance, and Windows-based troubleshooting.

titles.title: Business Systems Analyst
titles.description: Analyzes business needs and translates them into system requirements, process improvements, and functional specifications. Works with users and technical teams to support application changes and enhancements.

titles.title: Application Support Analyst
titles.description: Supports business applications by troubleshooting user issues, monitoring application health, and coordinating fixes or enhancements. Works with developers, vendors, and users to keep applications functioning properly.

titles.title: Systems Support Analyst
titles.description: Investigates and resolves issues affecting systems, applications, and related services. Monitors incidents, documents solutions, and helps maintain stable day-to-day operations.

titles.title: Enterprise Systems Analyst
titles.description: Analyzes enterprise-wide systems and business processes to define requirements and improve system performance or integration. Works across departments to support large-scale technology solutions.

titles.title: Information Security Analyst
titles.description: Monitors and analyzes security events, vulnerabilities, and control effectiveness to protect systems and data. Supports incident response, risk identification, and security reporting.

titles.title: Network Engineer
titles.description: Designs, implements, and troubleshoots network infrastructure such as routing, switching, firewalls, and connectivity services. Works on network performance, resilience, and secure architecture changes.

titles.title: Cybersecurity Compliance Manager
titles.description: Manages activities that ensure security controls meet internal policies and external regulatory requirements. Oversees assessments, remediation tracking, and compliance reporting.

titles.title: Cybersecurity Engineer
titles.description: Builds and maintains technical security solutions such as endpoint protection, monitoring tools, identity controls, and network defenses. Implements security architecture and resolves complex protection issues.

titles.title: Cybersecurity Program Manager
titles.description: Coordinates cybersecurity initiatives across multiple projects, teams, and stakeholders. Tracks schedules, risks, deliverables, and governance for security programs.

titles.title: IT Systems Manager
titles.description: Leads teams that administer and support core IT systems and services. Oversees maintenance, upgrades, incident response, and operational performance.

titles.title: Information Security Analyst
titles.description: Monitors and analyzes security events, vulnerabilities, and control effectiveness to protect systems and data. Supports incident response, risk identification, and security reporting.

titles.title: Security Compliance Analyst
titles.description: Evaluates whether security controls and practices meet policy, audit, and regulatory requirements. Collects evidence, identifies gaps, and tracks remediation actions.

titles.title: Cybersecurity Risk Analyst
titles.description: Analyzes cyber threats, vulnerabilities, and control gaps to determine risk to the organization. Supports risk documentation, mitigation tracking, and reporting.

titles.title: Risk and Compliance Analyst
titles.description: Reviews processes and controls to identify risk exposure and confirm compliance with policies or regulations. Prepares assessments, documentation, and remediation tracking for management.

titles.title: Software Developer
titles.description: Designs, codes, tests, and maintains software applications or features. Works with users and technical teams to build and improve functionality.

titles.title: Application Support Specialist
titles.description: Provides operational support for business applications, including troubleshooting, user assistance, and issue escalation. Helps keep applications available and usable for end users.

titles.title: Cybersecurity Compliance Analyst
titles.description: Checks security controls and documentation against policy, regulatory, and audit requirements. Supports evidence collection, gap analysis, and remediation follow-up.

titles.title: Enterprise Applications Manager
titles.description: Leads support and administration for major enterprise applications such as ERP, CRM, or workflow systems. Coordinates upgrades, user support, vendor management, and system improvements.

titles.title: Information Security Specialist
titles.description: Supports security operations and control implementation to protect systems and data. Assists with monitoring, documentation, awareness, and remediation of security issues.

titles.title: Security Compliance Specialist
titles.description: Helps ensure security practices align with internal standards and external requirements. Collects evidence, tracks corrective actions, and supports audits or assessments.

titles.title: Cybersecurity Specialist
titles.description: Performs a mix of security operations, control implementation, and issue resolution to protect systems and data. May support monitoring, hardening, awareness, or incident response tasks.

titles.title: Risk and Compliance Specialist
titles.description: Supports risk assessments, control testing, and compliance documentation for technology or security programs. Helps track findings, remediation, and audit readiness.

titles.title: Network Infrastructure Engineer
titles.description: Designs and supports the underlying network infrastructure, including routing, switching, firewalls, and connectivity services. Focuses on performance, resilience, and secure network operations.

titles.title: Network Support Specialist
titles.description: Provides operational support for network users and devices by troubleshooting connectivity, access, and configuration issues. Assists with routine maintenance and escalation of network problems.

titles.title: SharePoint Administrator
titles.description: Manages SharePoint sites, permissions, content structures, and platform settings. Supports site owners, resolves access issues, and performs maintenance or upgrades.

titles.title: Software Development Manager
titles.description: Leads software development teams responsible for building and maintaining applications. Oversees delivery schedules, technical quality, staffing, and coordination with business stakeholders.

titles.title: Systems Support Specialist
titles.description: Troubleshoots and resolves issues with systems, applications, and related services. Provides user support, documents fixes, and helps maintain stable operations.

titles.title: Application Developer
titles.description: Builds and maintains software applications, features, and integrations based on user and business requirements. Writes code, tests changes, and fixes defects.

titles.title: Cloud Systems Administrator
titles.description: Administers cloud-based servers, services, and user access in platforms such as Azure or AWS. Handles provisioning, monitoring, patching, and troubleshooting of cloud environments.

titles.title: Communications Systems Manager
titles.description: Manages systems that support voice, messaging, conferencing, or other communications services. Oversees reliability, upgrades, vendor coordination, and user support for communications platforms.

titles.title: Cyber Defense Manager
titles.description: Leads teams and tools focused on detecting, analyzing, and responding to cyber threats. Oversees defensive operations, incident handling, and threat mitigation activities.

titles.title: Cybersecurity Compliance Specialist
titles.description: Supports compliance efforts by checking security controls against policies, standards, and regulations. Helps gather evidence, document findings, and track remediation tasks.

titles.title: Database Administrator
titles.description: Maintains database systems by managing performance, backups, security, availability, and recovery. Troubleshoots database issues and supports application teams with data access and tuning.

titles.title: End User Computing Specialist
titles.description: Supports the devices, software, and services employees use to work, such as laptops, desktops, mobile devices, and collaboration tools. Handles deployment, configuration, and troubleshooting of user endpoints.

titles.title: Enterprise Systems Manager
titles.description: Leads administration and support for large-scale enterprise systems and platforms. Coordinates upgrades, operations, vendor support, and service improvements across the organization.

titles.title: IT Security Director
titles.description: Directs the organization’s information security strategy, operations, and governance at a senior level. Oversees security teams, risk management, compliance, and executive reporting.

titles.title: IT Support Manager
titles.description: Manages the team that provides technical support to end users and business units. Oversees ticket queues, service levels, staffing, and escalation of complex issues.

titles.title: IT Systems Support Specialist
titles.description: Provides technical support for systems, applications, and user access issues. Troubleshoots incidents, performs routine maintenance, and escalates problems when needed.

titles.title: Information Security Manager
titles.description: Leads a team or function responsible for protecting systems and data through security controls, monitoring, and incident response. Oversees security policies, risk reduction efforts, and compliance activities.

titles.title: Security Compliance Manager
titles.description: Manages programs that ensure security controls meet policy, audit, and regulatory requirements. Oversees assessments, remediation, and compliance reporting.

titles.title: Cybersecurity Manager
titles.description: Manages cybersecurity operations, controls, and staff responsible for protecting systems and data. Oversees threat response, security tooling, and implementation of security requirements.

titles.title: Risk and Compliance Manager
titles.description: Leads risk and compliance activities, including assessments, control testing, and remediation tracking. Ensures technology or security practices align with policy and regulatory expectations.

titles.title: Information Security Specialist
titles.description: Supports security operations and control implementation to protect systems and data. Assists with monitoring, documentation, awareness, and remediation of security issues.

titles.title: Information Systems Manager
titles.description: Oversees teams and services that support business information systems and related technology operations. Manages priorities, maintenance, upgrades, and user support for systems in use.

titles.title: Linux Systems Administrator
titles.description: Administers Linux servers and related services, including user accounts, patching, monitoring, and troubleshooting. Maintains system availability and supports application hosting environments.

titles.title: Litigation Support Specialist
titles.description: Supports legal teams with technology used for e-discovery, document management, and case preparation. Organizes data, manages review tools, and helps troubleshoot litigation support systems.

titles.title: Network Security Administrator
titles.description: Configures and maintains security controls on network devices and services such as firewalls, VPNs, and access rules. Monitors network security, investigates issues, and applies protective changes.

titles.title: Network Security Analyst
titles.description: Monitors network traffic and security alerts to identify threats, anomalies, and policy violations. Supports incident response, log analysis, and network security reporting.

titles.title: Network Security Engineer
titles.description: Designs and implements secure network solutions such as firewalls, segmentation, VPNs, and intrusion prevention. Troubleshoots complex security issues and improves network defenses.

titles.title: Server Administrator
titles.description: Maintains physical and virtual servers, including patching, backups, monitoring, and account administration. Troubleshoots server issues and supports availability of hosted services.

titles.title: Software Engineer
titles.description: Designs, develops, tests, and maintains software systems using engineering practices. Works on application architecture, code quality, debugging, and integration with other systems.

titles.title: Systems Engineer
titles.description: Designs, integrates, and supports complex IT systems across servers, networks, storage, and applications. Focuses on reliability, performance, interoperability, and technical problem solving.

titles.title: Systems Integration Manager
titles.description: Leads efforts to connect and coordinate multiple systems, applications, and data flows. Oversees integration projects, technical standards, and cross-team implementation work.

titles.title: Technical Project Manager
titles.description: Manages technical projects by coordinating schedules, resources, risks, and deliverables. Works closely with engineers and stakeholders to deliver technology solutions on time.

titles.title: Technical Support Specialist
titles.description: Provides troubleshooting and user support for hardware, software, and connectivity issues. Resolves routine technical problems and escalates more complex incidents.

titles.title: Technical Support Supervisor
titles.description: Supervises technical support staff and oversees daily service desk or support operations. Manages workloads, escalations, service quality, and team performance.

titles.title: Unified Communications Engineer
titles.description: Designs, implements, and supports voice, video, messaging, and collaboration platforms. Troubleshoots communications systems and ensures reliable service for users.

titles.title: Application Support Analyst
titles.description: Supports business applications by troubleshooting user issues, monitoring application health, and coordinating fixes or enhancements. Works with developers, vendors, and users to keep applications functioning properly.

titles.title: Cloud Security Architect
titles.description: Designs secure cloud environments, controls, and architectures for applications and infrastructure. Defines security patterns for identity, network, data protection, and monitoring in cloud platforms.

titles.title: Communications Systems Technician
titles.description: Installs, maintains, and troubleshoots communications equipment and services such as phones, conferencing, and related hardware. Performs routine repairs, configuration, and user support.

titles.title: Cyber Risk Manager
titles.description: Leads assessment and management of cyber risks across systems, data, and operations. Oversees risk analysis, mitigation planning, and reporting to leadership.

titles.title: Cybersecurity Architect
titles.description: Designs the overall security architecture for systems, networks, applications, and cloud environments. Defines security standards, control patterns, and technical requirements for secure solutions.

titles.title: Cybersecurity Assessor
titles.description: Evaluates security controls, systems, and processes to determine whether they meet required standards. Performs assessments, documents findings, and recommends corrective actions.

titles.title: Cybersecurity Project Manager
titles.description: Manages cybersecurity projects such as tool deployments, control implementations, or remediation efforts. Coordinates schedules, stakeholders, risks, and deliverables for security initiatives.

titles.title: Cybersecurity Risk Analyst
titles.description: Analyzes cyber threats, vulnerabilities, and control gaps to determine risk to the organization. Supports risk documentation, mitigation tracking, and reporting.

titles.title: Enterprise Architect
titles.description: Defines the organization’s high-level technology architecture across business capabilities, applications, data, infrastructure, and security. Aligns technology standards and roadmaps with enterprise strategy.

titles.title: Enterprise IT Director
titles.description: Directs enterprise-wide IT services, strategy, and major operational functions. Oversees managers, budgets, priorities, and alignment of technology with organizational goals.

titles.title: IT Acquisition Manager
titles.description: Manages the acquisition of IT products and services, including requirements, vendor coordination, and procurement support. Ensures purchases meet technical, budget, and policy needs.

titles.title: IT Asset Manager
titles.description: Tracks the lifecycle of IT hardware, software, and licenses from purchase through retirement. Manages inventory, compliance, renewals, and asset reporting.

titles.title: IT Director
titles.description: Directs IT strategy, operations, staffing, and major technology initiatives for an organization or division. Oversees budgets, service delivery, and alignment with business goals.

titles.title: IT Infrastructure Specialist
titles.description: Supports servers, networks, storage, cloud, and other foundational technology components. Performs maintenance, troubleshooting, and operational tasks to keep infrastructure reliable.

titles.title: IT Policy Manager
titles.description: Develops and manages IT policies, standards, and governance processes. Ensures technology practices align with organizational rules, risk requirements, and compliance needs.

titles.title: IT Service Delivery Manager
titles.description: Oversees delivery of IT services to users and business units, focusing on service levels, customer satisfaction, and operational performance. Coordinates support teams, incident resolution, and continuous improvement.

titles.title: IT Systems Architect
titles.description: Designs the structure and integration of IT systems, platforms, and services. Defines technical standards and solution patterns to meet business and operational requirements.

titles.title: IT Systems Engineer
titles.description: Builds, configures, and supports systems infrastructure and related services. Works on implementation, troubleshooting, automation, and performance improvements.

titles.title: Infrastructure Support Engineer
titles.description: Supports servers, storage, networks, and other infrastructure components through monitoring, troubleshooting, and maintenance. Resolves operational issues and assists with upgrades or changes.

titles.title: Network Administrator
titles.description: Configures and maintains network devices and services such as routers, switches, firewalls, and VPNs. Monitors network health, troubleshoots outages, and applies changes to keep connectivity stable.

titles.title: Network Support Specialist
titles.description: Provides operational support for network users and devices by troubleshooting connectivity, access, and configuration issues. Assists with routine maintenance and escalation of network problems.

titles.title: Network Operations Administrator
titles.description: Monitors and maintains network infrastructure to ensure availability and performance. Handles routine changes, incident response, and operational troubleshooting.

titles.title: Network Infrastructure Administrator
titles.description: Administers core network infrastructure including routing, switching, firewalls, and related services. Performs maintenance, monitoring, and troubleshooting to keep the network stable.

titles.title: Legal Technology Specialist
titles.description: Supports legal staff with technology tools for document management, e-discovery, case preparation, and litigation workflows. Troubleshoots legal software and helps manage case-related data systems.

titles.title: Network Operations Engineer
titles.description: Engineers and supports network operations processes, monitoring, and infrastructure changes. Troubleshoots complex network issues and improves reliability and performance.

titles.title: Network Support Technician
titles.description: Provides hands-on support for network equipment, cabling, and connectivity issues. Assists with installations, repairs, and basic troubleshooting of network problems.

titles.title: Network Systems Engineer
titles.description: Designs, implements, and supports networked systems and infrastructure components. Focuses on integration, performance, security, and troubleshooting across the network environment.

titles.title: SharePoint Developer
titles.description: Builds and customizes SharePoint sites, workflows, forms, and integrations. Develops solutions that improve collaboration, document management, and business processes.

titles.title: Systems Engineering Manager
titles.description: Leads systems engineering teams responsible for designing, integrating, and supporting complex IT environments. Oversees technical planning, project execution, and operational improvements.

titles.title: Systems Security Administrator
titles.description: Administers security settings and controls on systems, servers, and user accounts. Manages access, patching, hardening, and routine security maintenance.

titles.title: Systems Support Analyst
titles.description: Investigates and resolves issues affecting systems, applications, and related services. Monitors incidents, documents solutions, and helps maintain stable day-to-day operations.

titles.title: Technical Support Analyst
titles.description: Analyzes and resolves technical issues reported by users or detected in systems. Provides troubleshooting, documentation, and escalation support for hardware, software, and connectivity problems.

titles.title: Vulnerability Management Analyst
titles.description: Identifies, tracks, and prioritizes vulnerabilities across systems and applications. Coordinates scanning, remediation follow-up, and reporting on risk reduction efforts.

titles.title: AI Solutions Architect
titles.description: Designs AI-enabled solutions and the technical architecture needed to deploy them securely and effectively. Works on model integration, data flow, platform selection, and implementation standards.

titles.title: Analytics Engineer
titles.description: Builds and maintains data models, pipelines, and transformation layers used for reporting and analytics. Ensures data is reliable, well-structured, and ready for business intelligence use.

titles.title: Application Development Supervisor
titles.description: Supervises developers who build and maintain software applications. Coordinates work assignments, code quality, delivery timelines, and technical problem resolution.

titles.title: Application Support Manager
titles.description: Manages teams that support business applications and resolve user issues. Oversees incident handling, vendor coordination, upgrades, and service quality.

titles.title: Business Systems Analyst
titles.description: Analyzes business needs and translates them into system requirements, process improvements, and functional specifications. Works with users and technical teams to support application changes and enhancements.

titles.title: Chief Information Officer
titles.description: Sets overall technology strategy and leads the organization’s IT function at the executive level. Oversees budgets, governance, service delivery, and alignment of technology with mission goals.

titles.title: Cloud Security Manager
titles.description: Leads security efforts for cloud environments, including controls, monitoring, and risk management. Oversees cloud security policies, incident response, and compliance requirements.

titles.title: Cloud Solutions Architect
titles.description: Designs cloud-based technology solutions that meet business, security, and performance requirements. Selects services, defines architecture, and guides implementation in cloud platforms.

titles.title: Computer Systems Technician
titles.description: Installs, maintains, and repairs computer hardware, peripherals, and basic software configurations. Provides hands-on support for device setup and troubleshooting.

titles.title: Customer Support Manager
titles.description: Manages customer-facing support operations and staff who handle service requests and issue resolution. Tracks service levels, escalations, and customer satisfaction.

titles.title: Cyber Operations Manager
titles.description: Leads operational cyber teams that monitor, detect, and respond to threats. Oversees incident handling, defensive tooling, and operational readiness.

titles.title: Cyber Threat Analyst
titles.description: Researches and analyzes threat intelligence, attacker behavior, and indicators of compromise. Supports detection, incident response, and threat reporting.

titles.title: Cybersecurity Operations Manager
titles.description: Manages day-to-day cybersecurity operations such as monitoring, alert triage, and incident response. Coordinates staff, tools, and procedures to protect systems and data.

titles.title: Cybersecurity Policy Manager
titles.description: Develops and manages cybersecurity policies, standards, and governance processes. Ensures security requirements are documented, communicated, and aligned with organizational risk needs.

titles.title: Cybersecurity Systems Administrator
titles.description: Administers security-related systems and tools such as endpoint protection, logging, identity, and monitoring platforms. Performs configuration, maintenance, and troubleshooting of security technologies.

titles.title: Cybersecurity Systems Engineer
titles.description: Designs and supports technical security systems and integrations across the enterprise. Implements security tooling, automation, and architecture improvements to strengthen defenses.

titles.title: Cybersecurity Team Lead
titles.description: Coordinates the work of cybersecurity staff and helps prioritize daily security tasks. Provides technical guidance, handles escalations, and supports team execution of security operations.

titles.title: Data Architect
titles.description: Designs the structure, standards, and integration patterns for enterprise data systems. Defines how data is stored, moved, governed, and used across applications and analytics.

titles.title: Data Integration Manager
titles.description: Leads teams that connect data sources, pipelines, and platforms across the organization. Oversees integration design, data quality, and delivery of reliable data flows.

titles.title: Data Management Specialist
titles.description: Maintains data quality, definitions, governance, and lifecycle practices across systems. Supports data standards, documentation, and stewardship activities.

titles.title: Data Systems Manager
titles.description: Manages systems and teams responsible for storing, processing, and delivering data. Oversees operations, maintenance, and improvements to data platforms and services.

titles.title: Database Systems Administrator
titles.description: Administers database platforms by managing performance, backups, security, and availability. Troubleshoots database issues and supports application and reporting needs.

titles.title: DevOps Engineer
titles.description: Builds automation and deployment pipelines that connect development and operations work. Supports infrastructure as code, continuous integration, monitoring, and release reliability.

titles.title: End User Computing Manager
titles.description: Leads support for employee devices, desktop platforms, collaboration tools, and endpoint services. Oversees deployments, standards, troubleshooting, and user experience improvements.

titles.title: Enterprise Applications Analyst
titles.description: Analyzes enterprise application needs, workflows, and system behavior to support improvements and issue resolution. Works with users, vendors, and technical teams on enhancements and troubleshooting.

titles.title: Enterprise Network Engineer
titles.description: Designs and supports large-scale network infrastructure across the enterprise. Focuses on routing, switching, security, performance, and high availability.

titles.title: Firewall Administrator
titles.description: Configures and maintains firewall rules, policies, and related security settings. Monitors traffic, troubleshoots access issues, and supports network protection efforts.

titles.title: Full Stack Developer
titles.description: Builds both front-end and back-end parts of web or software applications. Writes code, tests features, and integrates user interfaces with server-side logic and data services.

titles.title: Help Desk Technician
titles.description: Provides first-line technical support for users with hardware, software, account, and connectivity issues. Logs tickets, resolves common problems, and escalates more complex cases.

titles.title: IT Asset Manager
titles.description: Tracks the lifecycle of IT hardware, software, and licenses from purchase through retirement. Manages inventory, compliance, renewals, and asset reporting.

titles.title: IT Asset Management Specialist
titles.description: Maintains records and processes for IT hardware, software, and license assets. Supports inventory tracking, audits, assignments, and disposal or replacement workflows.

titles.title: Technology Asset Manager
titles.description: Oversees the inventory, lifecycle, and compliance of technology assets across the organization. Coordinates procurement, tracking, renewals, and retirement of equipment and software.

titles.title: Hardware Asset Manager
titles.description: Manages the lifecycle of physical IT equipment such as laptops, servers, and peripherals. Tracks inventory, assignments, maintenance, and disposal or replacement.

titles.title: IT Customer Support Director
titles.description: Directs customer-facing IT support operations and service delivery. Oversees support teams, service levels, escalation handling, and user satisfaction initiatives.

titles.title: IT Hardware Deployment Specialist
titles.description: Prepares, configures, and deploys computers and other hardware to users or sites. Handles imaging, installation, asset tagging, and setup of standard equipment.

titles.title: IT Hardware Support Specialist
titles.description: Troubleshoots and repairs IT hardware such as desktops, laptops, printers, and peripherals. Performs maintenance, replacement, and hands-on device support.

titles.title: IT Policy Analyst
titles.description: Researches, drafts, and evaluates IT policies, standards, and governance documents. Helps ensure technology practices align with organizational rules and compliance needs.

titles.title: IT Portfolio Manager
titles.description: Manages the collection of IT projects and initiatives to align investments with strategic priorities. Evaluates demand, resource allocation, risks, and performance across the portfolio.

titles.title: IT Project Coordinator
titles.description: Supports IT project managers by tracking tasks, schedules, documentation, and communications. Helps keep projects organized and moving by coordinating meetings, updates, and follow-up actions.

titles.title: IT Security Compliance Analyst
titles.description: Assesses whether IT security controls meet policy, audit, and regulatory requirements. Collects evidence, documents gaps, and tracks remediation efforts.

titles.title: IT Service Desk Lead
titles.description: Leads service desk staff who handle user incidents and requests. Manages ticket flow, escalations, service quality, and day-to-day support operations.

titles.title: IT Support Technician
titles.description: Provides hands-on technical support for users, devices, and common software or connectivity issues. Installs equipment, resolves routine problems, and escalates complex incidents.

titles.title: IT Team Lead
titles.description: Coordinates the work of an IT support or operations team and provides technical guidance. Assigns tasks, handles escalations, and helps ensure service delivery targets are met.

titles.title: Identity and Access Management Specialist
titles.description: Manages user identities, access requests, and account provisioning across systems and applications. Supports access controls, role assignments, and identity lifecycle processes.

titles.title: Access Management Specialist
titles.description: Processes and maintains user access to systems, applications, and data based on approved requests and policies. Reviews permissions, resolves access issues, and supports access controls.

titles.title: Identity Governance Specialist
titles.description: Supports governance processes for user identities, roles, and access reviews. Helps ensure access is appropriate, documented, and periodically certified.

titles.title: Privileged Access Management Specialist
titles.description: Manages elevated accounts and privileged access controls for sensitive systems. Configures vaulting, approvals, monitoring, and periodic review of high-risk access.

titles.title: Identity Governance Analyst
titles.description: Analyzes identity and access data to support governance, certification, and compliance activities. Reviews roles, permissions, and access exceptions for risk and policy alignment.

titles.title: Information Security Administrator
titles.description: Administers security tools, settings, and user access to protect systems and data. Performs routine maintenance, monitoring, and troubleshooting of security controls.

titles.title: Information Security Architect
titles.description: Designs security architecture and standards for systems, applications, networks, and cloud environments. Defines control requirements and secure design patterns for technology solutions.

titles.title: Information Security Auditor
titles.description: Evaluates security controls, processes, and evidence to determine compliance with standards and requirements. Documents findings, risks, and recommendations for remediation.

titles.title: Information Security Officer
titles.description: Oversees information security governance, policy implementation, and risk management for an organization or program. Coordinates security initiatives, compliance, and incident response oversight.

titles.title: Security Compliance Officer
titles.description: Ensures security practices and controls meet policy, regulatory, and audit requirements. Oversees assessments, documentation, and corrective action tracking.

titles.title: Cybersecurity Compliance Manager
titles.description: Manages activities that ensure security controls meet internal policies and external regulatory requirements. Oversees assessments, remediation tracking, and compliance reporting.

titles.title: Information Security Manager
titles.description: Leads a team or function responsible for protecting systems and data through security controls, monitoring, and incident response. Oversees security policies, risk reduction efforts, and compliance activities.

titles.title: Information Security Specialist
titles.description: Supports security operations and control implementation to protect systems and data. Assists with monitoring, documentation, awareness, and remediation of security issues.

titles.title: Cybersecurity Specialist
titles.description: Performs a mix of security operations, control implementation, and issue resolution to protect systems and data. May support monitoring, hardening, awareness, or incident response tasks.

titles.title: Security Compliance Specialist
titles.description: Helps ensure security practices align with internal standards and external requirements. Collects evidence, tracks corrective actions, and supports audits or assessments.

titles.title: Systems Security Specialist
titles.description: Supports the security of servers, operating systems, and related infrastructure through hardening, monitoring, and access control. Helps investigate issues and maintain secure configurations.

titles.title: Infrastructure Systems Administrator
titles.description: Maintains core infrastructure systems such as servers, storage, virtualization, and related services. Handles monitoring, patching, troubleshooting, and routine operational support.

titles.title: Infrastructure Systems Engineer
titles.description: Designs, implements, and supports infrastructure systems across servers, storage, virtualization, and cloud components. Focuses on reliability, scalability, and technical problem solving.

titles.title: Machine Learning Engineer
titles.description: Builds and deploys machine learning models and the pipelines that support them. Works on data preparation, model integration, performance tuning, and production reliability.

titles.title: Network Architect
titles.description: Designs the overall structure of enterprise networks, including segmentation, routing, security, and resilience. Sets standards and roadmaps for network technology and evolution.

titles.title: Network Infrastructure Manager
titles.description: Leads teams responsible for network infrastructure operations, upgrades, and reliability. Oversees performance, maintenance, security, and vendor coordination.

titles.title: Network Operations Specialist
titles.description: Monitors and supports network services to keep connectivity stable and available. Handles routine troubleshooting, incident response, and operational maintenance.

titles.title: Network Security Specialist
titles.description: Supports the protection of network environments through monitoring, configuration, and troubleshooting of security controls. Assists with firewall rules, access issues, and threat detection.

titles.title: Network Systems Technician
titles.description: Installs, maintains, and troubleshoots network hardware, cabling, and connectivity issues. Provides hands-on support for network equipment and basic repairs.

titles.title: Oracle Database Administrator
titles.description: Administers Oracle database environments by managing performance, backups, security, and recovery. Troubleshoots database issues and supports application teams with data access and tuning.

titles.title: Power Platform Developer
titles.description: Builds business applications, workflows, and automations using Microsoft Power Platform tools. Creates solutions with Power Apps, Power Automate, and related services.

titles.title: SQL Server Database Administrator
titles.description: Maintains SQL Server databases by handling backups, performance tuning, security, and recovery. Supports application teams and resolves database-related issues.

titles.title: Security Compliance Analyst
titles.description: Evaluates whether security controls and practices meet policy, audit, and regulatory requirements. Collects evidence, identifies gaps, and tracks remediation actions.

titles.title: Security Operations Lead
titles.description: Leads day-to-day security operations such as alert triage, monitoring, and incident response. Coordinates analysts, tools, and procedures to detect and respond to threats.

titles.title: Identity and Access Management Engineer
titles.description: Designs and implements identity and access management solutions such as provisioning, authentication, and access controls. Integrates IAM tools with enterprise systems and automates access processes.

titles.title: Access Management Engineer
titles.description: Builds and supports technical access control solutions for user provisioning, permissions, and authentication. Works on integrations, automation, and troubleshooting access systems.

titles.title: Authentication Engineer
titles.description: Designs and maintains authentication systems such as single sign-on, multi-factor authentication, and federation. Ensures secure and reliable user sign-in across applications.

titles.title: Identity Security Engineer
titles.description: Implements technical controls that protect identities, credentials, and access pathways. Works on authentication, privileged access, and identity-related security integrations.

titles.title: Software Test Manager
titles.description: Leads testing efforts to verify software quality, functionality, and reliability. Oversees test planning, defect tracking, automation, and coordination with development teams.

titles.title: Systems Administration Supervisor
titles.description: Supervises systems administrators and oversees routine server and infrastructure operations. Assigns work, manages escalations, and ensures maintenance tasks are completed.

titles.title: Systems Integration Specialist
titles.description: Connects systems, applications, and data sources so they work together reliably. Builds interfaces, resolves integration issues, and supports data flow between platforms.

titles.title: Systems Requirements Analyst
titles.description: Gathers and documents technical and business requirements for systems and application changes. Works with stakeholders and engineers to define what the solution must do.

titles.title: Systems Security Engineer
titles.description: Designs and implements security controls for systems, servers, and infrastructure. Focuses on hardening, monitoring, access control, and technical risk reduction.

titles.title: Systems Support Manager
titles.description: Manages teams that support systems, applications, and infrastructure operations. Oversees incident response, service quality, staffing, and escalation handling.

titles.title: Technical Operations Manager
titles.description: Oversees technical operations that keep IT services and infrastructure running smoothly. Manages daily support, maintenance, performance, and operational improvements.

titles.title: Technology Program Manager
titles.description: Manages a set of related technology projects and initiatives as a coordinated program. Tracks dependencies, budgets, risks, and stakeholder communication across teams.

titles.title: Technology Support Director
titles.description: Directs technology support services across help desk, desktop, and operational support teams. Oversees service levels, staffing, escalation management, and customer experience.

titles.title: Voice Network Engineer
titles.description: Designs and supports voice communications networks such as VoIP and telephony systems. Troubleshoots call quality, routing, configuration, and integration issues.

titles.title: Web Applications Developer
titles.description: Builds and maintains web-based applications, user interfaces, and supporting server-side logic. Writes code, tests features, and fixes defects in browser-accessed systems.

titles.title: Windows Server Administrator
titles.description: Administers Windows Server environments, including patching, Active Directory, backups, and monitoring. Troubleshoots server and service issues to keep Microsoft infrastructure available.

titles.title: Windows Systems Engineer
titles.description: Designs, implements, and supports Windows-based infrastructure and services. Works on server architecture, automation, troubleshooting, and performance improvements.

titles.title: Wireless Network Engineer
titles.description: Designs, deploys, and troubleshoots wireless network infrastructure and access points. Focuses on coverage, performance, security, and connectivity for mobile users.
